paper.title: Gaia and the Galactic Center Origin of Hypervelocity Stars

extraction.summary: 39 HVS Survey radial velocity outliers (v_rf>+275 km/s) reassessed with Gaia DR2 proper motions. 3 additional stars (HVS14, HVS23, B149) lack Gaia measurements and are excluded. Stars classified into 3 categories: 7 Galactic Center HVSs (HVS1,4,5,6, B537, B598, +1), 7 disk runaways (including HVS7, HVS17, B733, B485, B434, B711, B576), and 4+ halo stars. All stars originally from HVS Survey (Brown+2007b, 2014); this paper uses Gaia DR2 + 10^6 MC trajectory calculations to determine origin. Table 2 provides v_rf, R, v_rf-v_esc, p_GC, p_Disk for all stars.

method_chain.summary: 42 radial velocity outliers from HVS Survey (Brown+2007b, 2014) with v_rf>+275 km/s. Gaia DR2 proper motions for 39 stars (3 missing: HVS14, HVS23, B149). HST proper motions for 14 stars, GPS1 for 33. Spectrophotometric distances from Padova main-sequence tracks (15% precision).
outputs: 39 stars with distances and proper motions

source_refs.context: Tab. 1: 42 stars with coordinates, Gaia/HST/GPS1 proper motions
Sec 2.1: 42 v_rf>+275 km/s outliers from HVS Survey

method_chain.summary: Kenyon+2014 3-component bulge-disk-halo model. Flat 235 km/s rotation curve, M_halo=10^12 Msun. Solar: R0=8 kpc. v_esc(R0)=580 km/s consistent with Monari+2018.
outputs: Galactic potential and escape velocity curve

method_chain.summary: 10^6 MC trajectory calculations per star. Backward integration through Galactic potential. Record disk crossing location and distance from GC. Correlated normal distributions for parallax, pmRA, pmDec via Cholesky decomposition of Gaia covariance matrix.
outputs: Trajectory density distributions at disk crossing

method_chain.summary: Origin classification: GC HVS (trajectory passes within GC), disk runaway (trajectory from outer disk), halo star (rejects both). Evaluate at 1σ/2σ/3σ thresholds. 18 stars with robust constraints. GC origin dominates at v_rf>+500 km/s; disk runaways at v_rf~v_esc; halo stars at v_rf<+300 km/s.

source_refs.context: Sec 5: 7 GC HVSs, 7 disk runaways, halo domination at low speeds

method_chain.summary: Effective escape velocity computed by dropping test particle from virial radius. Stars classified as unbound if v_rf > v_esc(R). Unbound: HVS1,4,5,6,7,17,19, etc. Bound: B-series stars + some HVS near escape.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS1: RA=9:07:45.0, Dec=2:45:07, g=19.79 mag. v_rf=669.8±6.6 km/s, R=106.9±15.3 kpc, v_rf-v_esc=421 km/s. p_GC=0.41959, p_Disk=0.13256. Classification: gc_hvs. Prototype HVS. Unbound, probable GC origin.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Stars originally from HVS Survey (Brown+2007b, 2014). This paper reassesses with Gaia DR2 proper motions using 10^6 MC trajectory calculations. Table 2 provides p_GC and p_Disk probabilities.
